title: sorbus umbellata subsp. baldaccii
intro: Sorbus umbellata subsp. baldaccii, also known as the mountain ash or whitebeam, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and is often used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk remedies in some parts of the world.
